2026-03-02.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports Producer VPC spokes with Network
Connectivity Center, a generally available feature. For more information, see
[Connect additional networks with NCC](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/get-started/quickstarts/connect-additional-networks#connect-additional-networks-with-ncc).

summary.title: Producer VPC Spokes Now Supported
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports Producer VPC spokes with Network Connectivity Center, a generally available feature. This enhancement allows for more flexible network configurations. For more details, please refer to the official documentation.

2026-02-25.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports Flex Unified large volumes in Preview, a
file-only solution with NFS and SMB protocols for massive datasets. This option
allows storage pools up to 20 PiB, and delivers up to 22 GiBps throughput and
750,000 IOPS. For more information, see
[Key features](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/discover/overview#key_features).

summary.title: New Large Volume Support in NetApp Volumes
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now offers Flex Unified large volumes in Preview. This file-only solution supports massive datasets with both NFS and SMB protocols, enabling storage pools up to 20 PiB and high performance.

2026-02-25.content:
The Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) for backup feature is generally
available for Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels, and in Preview
for the Flex Unified service level. For more information, see
[Backup encryption with CMEK](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/protect-data/about-backups#backup_encryption_with_cmek).

summary.title: Backup encryption with CMEK now available
summary.summary: The Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) for backup feature is now generally available for Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. It is also in Preview for the Flex Unified service level, offering enhanced data protection options.

2026-02-25.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports a new operational mode for Flex Unified
pools in Preview. ONTAP-mode provides direct API access to the underlying ONTAP
cluster, and supports both file and block volumes, and large volume pools. For
more information about managing different features, see
[About ONTAP-mode](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/ontap/overview#about_ontap-mode).

summary.title: New ONTAP-mode for NetApp Volumes
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now offers a new ONTAP-mode for Flex Unified pools in Preview. This mode grants direct API access to the ONTAP cluster and supports both file and block volumes, as well as large volume pools.

2026-02-25.content:
The block storage with iSCSI protocol is generally available for the Flex
Unified service level. For more information, see
[Block storage](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/discover/features#block_storage).

summary.title: Block storage now generally available
summary.summary: Block storage with iSCSI protocol is now generally available for the Flex Unified service level. This feature enhances storage flexibility and performance for your applications.

2026-02-25.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports Flex Unified File service level with both
NFS and SMB protocols in Preview. For more information, see
[Key features](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/discover/overview#key_features).

summary.title: Unified file service now in preview
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now offers Flex Unified File service in preview. This feature supports both NFS and SMB protocols, providing a flexible solution for your file storage needs.

2026-02-06.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports the all-squash feature for NFS exports.
This option lets you enhance security by mapping all client user IDs to a single
anonymous user ID (`UID=65534`). For more information, see [User ID squashing](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/connect-clients/connect-nfs-clients#user_id_squashing).

summary.title: NFS export security enhanced
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports the all-squash feature for NFS exports. This allows mapping all client user IDs to a single anonymous user ID, significantly improving security. This feature provides an extra layer of protection for your data.

2025-12-19.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) for
backup in allow-listed General Availability (GA). This feature is available for
Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. For more information, see
[Backup encryption with CMEK](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/protect-data/about-backups#backup_encryption_with_cmek).

summary.title: Backup Encryption with Customer Managed Keys
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) for backups across Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. This enhancement provides greater control over data protection for your backups.

2025-12-19.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports prepopulation and write-back for FlexCache.
For more information, see [Cache prepopulation](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/configure-and-use/volumes/cache-ontap-volumes/overview#cache_prepopulation)
and [Write-back](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/configure-and-use/volumes/cache-ontap-volumes/overview#write-back).

summary.title: FlexCache Enhancements for NetApp Volumes
summary.summary: NetApp Volumes now supports prepopulation and write-back for FlexCache. These features improve performance and data management for your cached volumes.

2025-11-21.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes offers volume replication between the following
region pairs for Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels:

* `europe-west4` (Netherlands) and `europe-west6` (Zurich)

For more information, see [About volume replication](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/protect-data/about-volume-replication).

summary.title: Volume Replication Availability
summary.summary: New volume replication is available between Netherlands and Zurich.

2025-11-06.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports the block storage feature with iSCSI
protocol in allow-listed General Availability (GA) for the Flex service level of
the **Unified** type. For more information, see
[Block storage](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/discover/features#block_storage).

summary.title: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes Now Supports iSCSI Block Storage
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes has introduced support for the iSCSI protocol, enabling block storage capabilities for the Flex service level of the Unified type. This feature is now generally available for allow-listed customers.

2025-11-12.content:
Google Cloud NetApp Volumes supports the all-squash feature for NFS exports.
This option lets you enhance security by mapping all client user IDs to a single
anonymous user ID (`UID=65534`). For more information, see [User ID
squashing](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/connect-clients/connect-nfs-clients#user_id_squashing).

summary.title: All-squash feature support
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports the all-squash feature for NFS exports, enhancing security by mapping all client user IDs to a single anonymous UID.

2025-10-01.content: The manual QoS feature is now generally available for Google Cloud NetApp Volumes, supporting the Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. For more information, see [Manual QoS](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/performance/optimize-performance#manual_qos).

summary.title: Manual QoS now generally available
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports manual QoS for Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels.

2025-09-26.content: Selective file restore feature is now generally available for Google Cloud NetApp Volumes, supporting the Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. For more information, see [Selective file restore](https://docs.cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/protect-data/about-backups#selective_file_restore).

summary.title: Selective File Restore Now Available
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now offers selective file restore for Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. This feature allows for more granular data recovery.

2025-09-24.content: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports the FlexCache feature in allow-listed General Availability (GA) for the Premium and Extreme service levels. For more information, see [FlexCache](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/discover/features#flexcache).

summary.title: FlexCache Feature Now Available
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports the FlexCache feature for Premium and Extreme service levels. This feature enhances data caching capabilities.

2025-09-05.content: The auto-tiering feature for the Flex service level is now generally available for custom-performance Flex zonal pools. For more information, see [Manage auto-tiering](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/configure-and-use/volumes/manage-auto-tiering).

summary.title: Auto-tiering for Flex is now available
summary.summary: The auto-tiering feature for the Flex service level is now generally available for custom-performance Flex zonal pools.

2025-08-25.content: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports the external replication feature in allow-listed General Availability (GA) for Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. This feature uses bi-directional SnapMirror to replicate data between ONTAP-based systems and NetApp Volumes. For more information, see [About external replication](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/protect-data/replicate-ontap/overview#about_external_replication).

summary.title: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports external replication
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes has released external replication, supporting data replication between ONTAP-based systems and NetApp Volumes using bi-directional SnapMirror.

2025-08-19.content: Large capacity volumes now support a maximum capacity of 3 PiB. For more information, see [Large capacity volumes](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/configure-and-use/volumes/overview#large-capacity-volumes).

summary.title: New large capacity volumes
summary.summary: Large capacity volumes now support a maximum capacity of 3 PiB.

2025-08-07.content: For the Flex service level, the storage pool and volume now support a maximum capacity of 300 TiB. For more information, see [Service levels](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/discover/service-levels).

summary.title: Increased capacity for Flex service level
summary.summary: The storage pool and volume for the Flex service level now support a maximum capacity of 300 TiB.

2025-07-31.content: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports organization policy for Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK). For more information, see [CMEK organization policy](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/configure-and-use/cmek/cmek-overview#cmek_organization_policy).

summary.title: New CMEK Organization Policy Support
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports organization policy for Customer Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK).

2025-07-29.content: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes is now integrated with NetApp BlueXP Connector. For more information, see [NetApp Volumes integration with BlueXP](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/discover/features#netapp-volumes-integration-with-bluexp).

summary.title: NetApp BlueXP Connector Integration
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes is now integrated with NetApp BlueXP Connector.

2025-07-25.content: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports cross-project cross-region replication for Standard, Premium, and Extreme service levels. This feature is generally available for allow-listed users. For more information, see [About volume replication](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/protect-data/about-volume-replication).

summary.title: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes Replication
summary.summary: Cross-project, cross-region replication for Google Cloud NetApp Volumes is now available.

2025-07-25.content: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports volume backups for large capacity volumes. This feature is now generally available. For more information, see [About backups](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/protect-data/about-backups).

summary.title: New Feature: Volume Backups for Large Capacity Volumes
summary.summary: Volume backups for large capacity volumes are now available for Google Cloud NetApp Volumes.

2025-07-22.content: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports multi-VPC peering for all service levels. For more information, see [Connect additional networks](https://cloud.google.com/netapp/volumes/docs/get-started/quickstarts/connect-additional-networks).

summary.title: Multi-VPC Peering Support
summary.summary: Google Cloud NetApp Volumes now supports multi-VPC peering for all service levels.
